title: pilosella × viridifolia subsp. rubricata
intro: Pilosella × viridifolia subsp. rubricata, also known as Red-headed False Sowthistle, is a rare and lesser-known plant species in the dandelion family that is native to certain regions of Europe. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Pilosella × viridifolia subsp. rubricata are still being studied, but it is believed to have potential in reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes due to its potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
